star_1.text: I checked my email, I also paid for the premium version of this app in Feb 2017. I'm also going to delete the app right before the 90 day free trial ends. I will not be bombarded with ads and I won't pay $10 a year for an app that I use less than 5 minutes a month. I have no interest in you asking me to email you, so you can tell me how the old app was from 2010 and new technologies had to be employed. Tech gets cheaper as years go by, not more expensive by 20 times or more. Do whats right!

I don't trust this developer. It left users of the previous PURCHASED version out in the cold with only an offer of an extended free trial. NO, I purchased this app with a one-time fee and I do not understand why they cannot honor these purchasers with a basic, ad-free experience. Install at your own risk and subscribe knowing that at anytime after you have paid, they will change the terms again and it's either pay up or get out. I don't see much more new in this version of the app than I did in the old. If you paid for the old and end up installing this one, you'll see pretty much the same thing with the exception of a huge ad at the top until you subscribe.

Stopped working completely. I loved this app and was going to purchase a sub or whatever a year ago, but then it stopped working. Updates didn't help, not better on a different phone. No point in paying for an app that doesn't work. update: adverts are intrusive and their X doesn't work, so there's no point in viewing them because you can't close them. Do better than the garbate scam adverts.

Flight Radar 24 service is getting worse and worse. If it is not persistent outages it is other issues. I love this app but this time they went too far. I have an active paid subscription for the Gold plan. Shorty after the monthly charge was taken out of my account they switched me back to basic. So, they took my money and ran basically. I paid for the service and I am not recieving what I paid for. I emailed customer service, they told me my account has expired but yet it shows paid for.

Works again after map hotfix but really, what's the point? As a purchaser of the former pro version, I, like all the others, have been abandoned by the developer when they upgraded the app to require a paid subscription. Now, to keep the benefits we had as an original purchaser, they want us to pay again. And again, and again, etc, etc, etc. No thanks!

I've been using FlightRadar24 for almost a decade. But after latest update, app is no longer what it used to be. Info that was previously freely accesible, now is only available through fee subscription. Also, now you are required to log in every simple time you want to look info up. Tracking a single flight for either take-off or arrival time takes way too many steps and has gotten cumbersome. You can get the same info using other apps such as Flight Aware.

So I paid for your "old" app's Pro version. Not a SUBSCRIPTION. Because of some notification issues (which I don't use) you're pushing me to download the "new" app (which I did), offering 3 free months of a subscription (which I don't want), and STILL only allow me to choose one label for flights!! In the version I paid for, I could choose up to 4 labels (I chose 3.) Leaves a bad aftertaste when a company, after taking your money, decides it will try to squeeze you for more. I don't like it.

Not a happy camper. No longer have access to extra features I paid for. What is going on? This is inexcusable, I paid for the extra features and should have been grandfathered into what ever newer versions are developed, afterall, without mine and thousands of other paying customers money, you wouldn't have the ability to develop new and improved versions.

Every time I open this app, I'm bombarded with a 'Your Privacy' pop-up 'We collect sensitive personal data and we share or sell it to third parties'. This happens even though I'm a paid gold subscriber! It feels like they're actively trying to get me to accidentally hit 'Agree and Close' instead of the 'Do Not Share, or Opt-out' button. To actually opt out, I have to go through a frustratingly long process that takes like 7 clicks! This is a dark pattern and a major privacy concern.

Don't waste your time with this app. They literally make the app slow so you're forced to buy a upgraded subscription. Not all of us are going to fork out 5-10 bucks a month to use a app they may only have open once or twice a month if that. Even the basic account is way to packed full of ads to a point it's unusable. Sorry, but I'll be using another app. TY.

Yet again, another disappointment. I pay for a subscription to use this app, and for the past couple of days it hasn't worked. I recieve notifications as if its working and for followed flights and emergencies, but when I open the app it just crashes. Between getting ripped off after buying the full version several years ago and now having to pay for a subscription, and now paying for things that don't work, Flightradar24 is dropping very quickly on my recommendations and favorite apps list.

I signed up for a 7 day trial of the Gold subscription. While using the app I noticed that a lot of information wasn't showing up on flights near my house, and the airport a few miles away didnt't show up. All of this info was on a free version of FlightAware. I decided to cancel the subscription in the google app store (as instructed in the app) but my credit card had already been charged even though the full 7 days was not up yet. I want a refund!

Destination is often missing. Origin is occasionally as well. When I check those same flights on FlightAware, they're not missing. Need to be able to show more text labels on the map for each flight than just aircraft type. Need to be able to view origin and destination on the arrival departure boards. Map type coloring needs improvement, especially the darker ones (hard to see details). Update: I'veso noticed some flights are just missing (but FA shows them), and wrong pictures.

Very disappointed that an App that I paid for just one day quits working forever. That is like a car manufacturer telling you "sorry your car is 10 years old and you'll need to buy a new one. We've disabled it" I've been using the paid Pro version since it originally came out and probably would have supported the move if given the choice. But not when its forced.

The advertisements that pop up and take over your entire screen make this app completely unusable. It is sad that this once-great app keeps getting worse and worse as time goes by. Look elsewhere Came back today to try it again. Ads are even more intrusive and longer. App freezes before showing ad. What a joke. Can you refund the money I paid years ago for the "pro" version, which I was very happy with? No, I'm not subscribing to be endlessly in debt.

I had to scroll down a long list to find the airline. This is not good. Also I had to enter airport codes rather than the city names. This is not a good feature either. Number three, the plane I wanted to track should be shown alone on the map rather than a gazillion of others. Why would I want to see all the other planes? That's why, i an uninstalling your app two minutes after i installed it.

I paid for the pro version, and I understand when you add new features that you have the right to charge a premium. However when you retard features and degrade performance, that's making the app less than what I bargained for when I purchased it. It seems you're getting a bit greedy. I hope others keep this in mind when they consider purchasing.

I had paid for the original app upgrade for a lifetime sub. Now that is all over and I have to pay a subscription fee every year. Love the app, but not the bait and switch. To top it off, I was offered three months of silver sub free but when I decided to bite the bullet and get a new sub, the app only gave me one week free. Another bait and switch.

Not only do you have ads everywhere in the app, making you money, but you lock down most everything you can, and only 3 uses of 3d! You were one of the first apps of this type, and you were a very good app, but you reward your customer base, which without them you would not succeed, by giving them nothing! You have like over a million downloads! You should reward customers for making you successful, not get more greedy! I'm sure subscription only would really pay! Uninstall

Used to be a great app. Paid for a lifetime license, which they did not honor when they switched to subscription based very shortly after. Now, not only does it nag you to subscribe, it pops up full screen, extra loud ads without warning, which also have no way to exit them short of swiping back to exit out. There are better apps out there.

star_2.text: I had the old app which I was very happy with and suddenly I was apparently paying for a monthly gold subscription without me accepting such a thing. Using the free version nowadays and the intrusive ads are extremely annoying, they'll open up their own page and then you have to restart Flightradar to be able to use it again.

I'll keep my old paid version. It does the job. Too many nags to update, too many ads. To get all the features is too expensive and it's an annual fee on top of that. Upgraded version is not for the causal user. Also, the squawk feature won't turn off even if unchecked. Annoying. Had some trouble trying to track back to back flights. It only kept the first flight active in the search box and would not load the second flight even after it was airborne until about an hour into its flight. The old version worked fine. The rest of the app works great and is enjoyable. But not for the price.

Tried the 1 week free trial, glad I didn't have to pay for it beforehand. Needed Filters & Alerts but couldn't get Alerts to work properly the whole week. The app should send an Alert when your chosen plane enters the map area selected. This would work on 1 of every 10 planes. The rest were on the ground when I got an alert even though I didn't adjust altitude settings to zero (or set them at all). Fix this and I'll be happy to pay your yearly fee.

After initial install it worked great but after about 45 days more and more adverts to subscribe for monthly fee. At same time flightradar became slower, less accurate, seemingly buggy in the free version. Standing within 5 ft of my neighbor with the most basic paid version the tracking was more accurate, the info displayed was much more accurate. He has a few 'enhancements' that the free version doesn't have but for almost $35 USD per year..? He says he won't recommend / nor is he renewing.

Until installing the latest update, I would have given this app a five-star rating. The new update limits the filter options so much, it's pointless. The filters I was using was simply altitude and speed. If someone can show me how to do that without having to pay for it, I'd greatly appreciate it. Or, better yet, help me uninstall the latest update.

Ive used the app/website for years. Free version has a good set of features that were good enough. the app/website interfaces vary...some info u can't get via the app. I signed up for a paid membership (more data). It got worse. I took a flight that disappeared. I know I landed so it didn't disappear. I took another flight after - it didn't show either. contact FR24 on Twitter. fast response sending me to support. after that it was a circus. still inconsistent data (fully shows on Flightaware)

SILVER MEMBER Was previously a 5* app. However, recently, in the last month, the data screen (which shows all air traffic) seems to take much longer to load. The "spinning, data-loading" circle appears nearly constantly. The air picture does eventually load but continues to lag sporadically. It seems almost as if there's a need to "clear cache" but I'm not sure if that's even possible. I've not made any changes/modifications to my device (Samsung S21), so I'm pretty sure it's not me.

Layout can be confusing. Has to be tagged Live before it can track. Even when flights are cruising, the Live tag didn't show, thus unable track. Can't find away to save flights to track to review later. Have to find the flight using the search feature everytime. 3D view is nice, but most of the time it's inaccurate. Setting alerts is tricky.

Used to work really well... now it's a laggy jumbled mess of icons because the option to sort by altitude is unavailable for free users. I get you gotta make money to maintain the app, but you gotta understand how badly you screwed up your app for a lot of users. I'm even reading the paid subscription version is really messed up according to other reviews, so why you as a company have the impression I would want to actually buy the paid subscription knowing that it's just as bad is beyond me.

Having purchased the old version of the app, now being told that it's going to be discontinued, just because FR24 wanted to change its business model is very disappointing. There are no technical limitations as to why they couldn't update the old paid app. A 3 month free trial is not worth very much. I will be finding a different source for ADSB aircraft tracking.

Great app for tracking flights BUT the adverts are just spoiling the experience and so annoying. When they come on, with no warning you can't get rid of them. TEMU are the wordt three adverts, one after another and no 'cancel' button. I understand money is involved, but they are so intrusive it's getting like youtube. Any chance you could tone the adverts down a touch banner adverts instead of full screen videos with sounds. Yes cash in but balance it and turn it down PLEASE How?

The app itself is great, but the ads are SO intrusive. It pops up a full screen video ad at full volume, which cannot be closed, and when you finally manage to close it, you find it's opened Play Store for the advertised product. Other banner ads also start at full volume, scaring the wits out of anyone nearby. There is no way I'm going to pay to remove the ads if the company treats us with such contempt. They could have got 5 stars, but the ads mean they only get 2 and no purchase.

ads are so intrusive. such poor development decisions being made with FR24. They could have made the app far more enjoyable had they considered how they deploy ads. instead they opt for full screen, barely unskippable ads when all you need to do is see flights quickly. I think the developers have forgotten that their data is supplied by volunteers across their network. deleting and moving elsewhere.

The intended app experience is fine but for users who don't care enough about flight tracking to pay for it, just use the browser version. This app employs the most offensive type of advertising strategy: full screen, loud audio, video ads where you have to tap a delayed X THREE separate times to leave the ad and it will still open the browser most of the time.

Sometimes, it shows flight status that differs from official airline / airport status, and I don't know which to believe... Also hosts misleading scare tactic scam ads, "Your phone needs to be updated Incompatible software version: This version of the tool is too old and does not support the latest PDF format."

Why is there always so many problems with this app???? I go on, nothing showing up anywhere. I delete the app, reinstall, now saying my log in is no good and can't log in. I am a gold member but canceling it now. I refuse to pay for an app that has this many problems.

The app used to be very accurate with flight times. However, the last two times I had to use it, the landing times of both flights were off by about 20 to 30 minutes which is a lot. It's better to use the airlines' sites or other apps if you want up-to-date information.

unprecise and a pain to use. ADnoyance pestered, poor performance. even the clunky homepage does better. Edit: the app runs worse than the homepage in every aspect. refresh rate of basic gfx and UI should be better in a dedicated app and isnt. entering flight number doesnt focus on the flight but shows ADnoyance ridden plane type info. delays arent shown correctly-if at all.

This many advertisements to upgrade is harassment, really intrusive and spoils the enjoyment of anotherwise good application. yes i know I can pay money and subscribe!! but the amount to use it It wouldn't be a benefit
Used to be good, but lately the app has serious bugs. Notifications that continue daily for a flight that ended weeks ago, and current flight status not up to date. Cancelled subscription.

star_3.text: This is a cool app with enough free features to keep you watching flights for hours. Haven't had any major problems. Not glitchy. Some ads but what do you expect these days. Edit: I have dropped two stars because while watching a flight long enough, an ad will automatically exit the app and redirect to your web browser. That's not acceptable and I will be deleting this app if that continues.

The app changed today. It was great before; I have had a gold subscription, but lost my filters. I don't remember any type of notification popping up or any type of email warning people of it. If there was, it wasn't enough for me to notice. I had mentioned it on X and their response was retweeting at me something they sent out on December 23rd. Update: The update is good overall, it covers a lot of my filters as "built in" filters anyway. But it still sucks I lost the one-offs.

Update June 14 2024: something has changed, maybe it's the free version, or maybe they no longer get accurate information. Several times in the past two months this App has said my American Airlines flight was on time when it clearly was not. I could see maybe 1-3 minutes off but it has been worse (yesterday over 30 min!!) The announcements at the gate are more reliable than this app now-a-days, it used to be the complete opposite. I guess I can still look at the pretty planes and ID them.

App is fine and new features are attractive. I am another former pro version user and am very disappointed that the other app is at its end of life (even though it's working just fine, it will now be turned off.) I think turning off ads should be a one-time payment of something reasonable like we paid for pro on the other app. If the new app will last ten years, the cheapest subscription will cost you $100 for those years-for silver level, as gold is $350. Subscription model is awful compared.

What have you done with the program???? I have been a gold member for some time and absolutely loved this app!!! Suddenly you have changed the graphics and I can't stand it! The aircraft icon is so small I can't tell the aircraft! What's worse is the display for flight # and destination is like 20x the size of the original program. The labels are so large and overlap so much that some aircraft completely disappear. PLEASE GO BACK !!! Can I find the old program any other way?

They have probably the most obnoxious screens that pop up asking if it's okay to sell your personal data. It is not at all intuitive how to get out of these screens, and I'm not certain I was even successful in opting out. Complicating that is their decision to place the Save button in a location which is hidden by the tool which expands and reduces the screen, making it all but invisible and impossible to access. Really sucky job for an app I pay a lot of money to use.

I really like this app - easy to use - I've had a subscription for many years. My biggest complaint is that I frequently have to switch to other ADSB providers as Flightradar24 can't see a large number of light aircraft that fly over. It also can't see military aircraft a lot of the time. It's frustrating to know that the providers are hiding certain kinds of flights.

Ruined for casual users.... Used to accept the lack of access and small advertisements due to not paying the subscription, however now you can't look at more than one aircraft without being transferred to temu without the ability to get back into the app. Completely ruined the experience for those who don't use it enough to justify paying.

1. Ads occasionally open on their own. I have my phone sitting on my desk with the app open and my browser suddenly opens to an ad's website. 2. The 15-second, full-screen ads are very frustrating when you're trying to quickly see a plane. The Close button also takes four to five seconds to fade onto the screen after the ad, so a 15-second ad ends up being 19 - 20 seconds.

while checking the flight status mid-flight, we got a scare as the radar showed the flight at 36000 ft but only doing 23 knots and it hadn't moved from that position. The radar showed it had turned back 40 minutes into the flight. Then it showed it on another path but the same flight details as before. Finally I checked on another radar site and found everything was OK.

idk why they want me paying for stuff on this. Anyway I installed this to monitor the mass amounts of chem trails being dumped over Europe but the planes that do so usually have their transponder off so they are putting themselves in ghost mode pretty much and the ones that do have their transponder turned on it doesn't give any information on where the aircraft comes from where it goes to land what plane it is literally nothing but thats all really so yeah other than that the app is okay 👍
I paid for the silver membership to access flight alerts. the alerts didnt work. I would see the specific flight above me and the alert would never activate on the app.
The full screen ads were bad enough but now you have Ali Express automatically opening a browser page without touching the screen. This isn't the first app I've seen doing this.
This App used to work normally, but in the last six months, it redirects to Chinese Websites such as Ali Express, Shein or Temu after a few seconds of using it. it's so annoying!
I just upgraded thru the 7day trial but only got two days in the trial before the fee was to be enacted. so i cancelled. I just want the 7 days before subbing
Love this app and I completely understand the need for ads but the unskipable 30+ second ones are brutal. Please come up with a better alternative.

Great App. Very easy to use. Very user friendly. I use it just for useless information purpose and I am enjoying very much. Now I know what is hapening in the air space above me. Legal flights and not so legal flights. Give some more free info and the App will get FIVE STARS. Now after I declined your offer to become a paid customer the App is so slow. Unbelievable. I am very disappointed.

This app is not life....I was stuck on a plane in Sydney for 3 hours and was not showing on the app. Also at the same time heaps of planes grounded in Sydney and showed the terminal with just one plane

A problem has been occurring where checking on an alert when the aircraft has landed already, you used to be able to check its flight path by clicking the message to see flight path. You no longer have this option.

Not usually one to complain but latest update of this app has ads that cannot be closed unless you force close the app. super super annoying, i don't mind ads for a free app but when I cannot close them that is really annoying. Have used this app for a long time and enjoy everything about it except this new ad feature.

star_4.text: I would say this is a good app to find flights, it can show planes nearby, and you can look up and see, but it has a few issues. For example, using the AR feature, it only works best when the plane is close, because the arrow is always pointing at the wrong place, and the box is covering the plane on camera. And in the feature, it has a tiny map with a slide to have the radar look farther for planes, but they always show the plane facing the wrong way, so I think it's going my way, but it isn't.

If you buy the top version there are lots more things available. But the free version let's me identify any airplane that is on active radar. It also allows me to track the flight if I care to. It gives me the N number, type of craft, altitude, ground speed, and usually point of origination. If you have a friend going on a commercial flight and they give you the flight number, you can track the flight. Fun and handy app.

Love that I can see what's flying over, with the exception of most military aircraft (which is understandable). Some info is occasionally missing from a few flights, a few have incorrect photos of the model of aircraft, but most have good info. I enjoy getting Featured Flight notifications - I was able to watch the last flight of Quantas 747 as it drew it's logo over the skies of Australia. I also like that I can get notified when my favourite birds are up, either by model or tail number.

Best free tracker I've ever used! Definitely the most accurate one, and being able to watch the icons taxiing, taking off, and landing is really cool. My only issue is that it seems to not be very precise on the takeoff and landing areas. The icons will freeze at the beginning of takeoff, overshoot the runway every landing, and the altitude indicator will continue to read 800ft after landing. Still the best free app.

Very detailed app when it comes to tracking flights, viewing history of the aircraft as far as where it's been, the age of the aircraft by showing how long it has been it service and multiple picture of the actual aircraft. my biggest gripe is probably a small one. The ability to save the track aircraft that you are looking for. To like a list. That has alerts to it I will notify you when an aircraft is went a specific range or on the ground.

I am really happy with the application and often spend hours in front of my computer looking at all the various different kinds of aircraft. I am a military aircraft buff and understand regarding the blockage of Military Jet aircraft transponders (USAF, USN, USMC and USCG and other jet aircraft). BUT, why aren't there photos attached/linked with Jetphotos if I can find the Aircraft (Registration) on Jetphotos? I've asked before but no answer. I LOVE the app!!!! I want to learn to use it better!

I'm out in the country where at night I hear planes and helicopters flying overhead. With This app I can see their identity speed, altitude, point of origin/destination, civilian or military, type of plane, cargo or passenger, Flight times and more. I zoom in on busy airports like JFK, NEWARK, LAGUARDIA out of the NYC area and am amazed at the order, precision and timing they operate at. Some flights go to the opposite side of the earth.

It does as good as you can expect. It does not put you on the planes exact location but pretty close. You are able to track most planes if you spot one in the air. It works very well on most airports departing and incoming flights. The only reason I gave it a 4 star was because you are not following the planes exact location. Thank you developers for the app. A job well done.

New updated filters are too confusing. You can't just quickly enter a plane type or airline and find it like the previous filters. After just about 4 years with a paid subscription I have just cancelled mine. Why do good apps always get destroyed with "upgrades". EDIT: The developer has reached out and shown me that although the old easier filters are gone, I can still use the new filters in a similar way. I will continue my subscription for this app as I find it useful and informational.

Its ok since it does show planes, but when I search the plane I want to track, it won't show me the flight path. This means that instead of just searching the plane/ number or anything to track, you have to check where it take off and lands, then press the random planes in-between take off and landing places

I "tried" the free trial but they made sure to charge for the free trial as there was hardly any way to disable the subscription inside the app. that surely feels like a scam , to make sure that cancelling is as hard and as inaccessible as possible . edit: despite the fraustration caused by the situation, the support team really acted fast and I'm really happy with the result

On the whole this is a great app for tracking friends and relatives flights if they are coming to visit, I have noticed recently that the app freezes when flights land, it seems like the plane hasn't taxied and is stuck on the runway.

it does what it needs to in a really efficient and cool way. only downgrade is looking for a specific area like your residential area, and stuff like that is really hard to do. also, having a pin feature for places in the map itself would really help. but other than that super cool

Further investigation shows that on opening the app after installation it takes 7 minutes to fully load up. this occurs on 2 different Samsung phones (A54 and A06). both are running android 16 with one UI 8.0. It works fine on a TCL T442A phone running android 14. one UI is not installed. once it's run once on the Samsung phones it works fine after.

Love to watch planes land and takeoff in 3D. Had the Silver option, but wasn't using it like I thought I would. The regular version sometimes has trouble loading the videos to watch which allows 3 more 3D views.
I like it and ads are pretty minimal but some stuff doesn't appear at all. Esp helicopters, I will see them in the sky or hear them and there's nothing.
Generally very good, although it would be really helpful to have a 'home' button to centre the map on the user's current location.
I think this is a lot more useful because we can spot planes in various locations using this app And the down side is they should give more options in free..
It is a good app and you can get some information about the plane but the problem is that sometime plane in radar vanishes and then for some minutes you can see the plane

I LOVE THIS APP! I can track our friends' flights coming home. I can see what the heck that extra loud noise is right over the house. I can even track our semi-frequent wildfires and see where the care-flights go. Thank you for this fun, informative, and useful app! The only reason I didn't give it 5 stars is because EYE am too dumb to understand it all!

star_5.text: I started using this app initially for the Skycard's game that is also by Flightradar24 to look at where different aircraft were but I've come to use it just about whenever I'm bored and find it fascinating! Especially with me, my family, and friends traveling as much as we all do, it's cool to see when planes are consistently late, on time, or even early. I would recommend this app for it's free version but I will admit that I got the Gold plan for $40 a year.

I have been using Flightradar24 for over 10 years, and the Android app for 5-10 years. It has always been my go-to flight tracker. Improvements are made frequently, and the app just keep getting better. It is not bogged down by including features not important to its basic premise. Help is supplied quickly and in a friendly fashion. I use some other flight trackers but always come back to Flightradar24. Keep up the good work!

Great app. Fast, clear, easy to use, and yes, the free version is quite usable, with small, unobtrusive ads. But if you really enjoy planespotting (and want to thank the devs), it's worth it to buy the full package of features (the weather options alone might even take the place of your current weather app). I paid for the full version, and I don't even fly. Terrified of flying, in fact. Hate it. But I really enjoy planespotting, and wouldn't be without Flightradar24.

I have used both free and paid versions of this app. In the free version, ads are not excessive, which I appreciate. There are different paid version tiers, and I settled on Silver for the flight data I like to see. $10 / year is a great price for what you get for this tier. It's also intuitive to use at a surface level, and there's more to explore if you want to dive deeper. All in all, excellent app!

I do car service from multiple airports. This app has improved to become the best choice for me. I can track specific flights, or see all the incoming traffic to a specific airport. The paid version is well worth the extra information, if you fly more than a couple times a year. The $10 a year subscription gives me all I need, for less than a buck a month. As I track a flight, the location on the map is very accurate. Then it tells me what gate my customers are deplaning at. Great app!

I am using the free version of this app to keep an eye on aircraft fighting wildfires in the area, and it works great for this. Sometimes the aircraft drops off view, but that's technology and probably terrain issues. My only regret, which is unavoidable, is that it requires data, so if i'm hiking in an area without service, there is no connection and aircraft can't be tracked. But that's not the fault of the app at all.

GREAT APP! Lets you track commercial flights worldwide along with most private aircraft and a few military. I'm about 30 miles from Fort Indian Town Gap in PA and can watch some of their training missions. I have the paid version which lets you see the ground below or ahead of the aircraft. It's a pretty cool option but eats up your data, so I only use it when I'm connected to Wifi. Even then, not much. It is pretty interesting, though, to watch the runway as they approach and land.

Absolutely awesome! If you've ever heard a plane or helicoptor, or looked up to see a jet contrail and wondered where it was going, came from, who owns it, what it actually looks like, how high/fast it is, etc., then I highly recommend this app. Very user-friendly, and accurate real time data for just about everything you'll see in the skies, including commercial, private, police, and most military transport aircraft. Only combat craft and a few old planes did not show up for obvious reasons.

It's always a great tool to have especially if you are a frequent flyer or you just love aviation. It's great when you can track your own flight if your plane has good WiFi signal and it's cool to see how accurate it is when your GPS works with the app. To get the best out of the app I recommend that you pay for it yearly look for the deals or promotions.

A super cool app. If ATC is tracking it, they'll have it on the app, including info like the n-number, which you can use to look up all the public info on that individual plane or helicopter. If it's military, they'll have its callsign. I've also found it to be the best among all the other apps that do the same thing. If you like watching planes, If you've ever seen something unusual fly over and thought "wow! I wonder what that is!" With this, you'll be able to find out.

I used the app for about a year before upgrading to the gold plan. It's a wonderful application that's really opened up my eyes to how aviation surrounds us. I've never run into any serious bugs, with the exception of the Atlantic tracks occasionally not showing. Hardly a deal-breaker. All told, this is a phenomenal application that I've recommended to probably a dozen or so others.

If you love flights, or if you love tracking, you MIGHT LIKE this app. But, if you love tracking flights, you'll LOVE this app! And just pay the couple bucks to get rid of the ads. Why skimp on that in times of $6 gasoline? Useful for people who travel by air a lot, of course, but for those of us who just like flying machines, it's a super fun way to identify every contraption passing over our heads.

I really like both the app and the website! I live near about five different airports, and I really enjoy being able to see what's going on in the air above me. I got the cheaper of the two membership prices, which was something like $14 or $15 for a year and is definitely worth getting. It applies to both the app and website, gets rid of ads, and unlocks a bunch of neat features. I'd say the more expensive one is for pilots since it has some of the finer details. Overall, great app!

I love this app!!! I am a pilot for a global airline and discovered your app when I was waiting on arrival of my aircraft in Tokyo. I noticed the ground staff periodically checking their phones and when I asked they pointed out that they could watch the progress of the inbound flight as it taxied across the airport. This allowed them to more accurately deploy ramp personnel to park aircraft. I immediately downloaded it and have found it incredibly useful, both professionally & personally.

So far the app has been awesome to use. So many options, customizations, and alerts. Works great for air traffic that has the specific radio to be tracked. Most of the traffic in my area doesn't show up on the radar. I haven't used the paid version or tried the trial so I don't know how well all the features do or don't work. If I knew more people that traveled or myself was more into traveling this would work great.

I tried a few versions of this type of app, comparing this to Air Traffic flight tracker, this one is superior, the other program wasn't at all accurate.   this one is. if i hear a plane over my house i can  see what plane it is, how high, speed, where it came from, where its going by seeing the line. what's cooler is that i can also see helicopters. though there seems to be a bug that shows a jet when it should be a helicopter or maybe vice versa?   now i know where those small planes came from, and i suppose i could complain to the correct airport asking them to move their flight path.   beyond that i'm not sure who this is for, other than seeing the hidden world of air traffic control.

Wow! I love this app. What I especially like is searching for aircraft in hot zones of the world that might seem unusual. I found an unidentified aircraft that left China this morning and rose to above 60,000 feet in the South China Sea where a U.S. aircraft carrier arrived within the last few days. It suddenly went off the map as it rose well above tracking elevation, I presume. It's what you can't see when you search that is very interesting sometimes. However, after only one day on this app, I'm sold. Glad I purchased the gold version. Well worth it.

I don't have the premium subscription, but it's still amazing. I always open it when I see an aircraft overhead, and there's an ar feature that lets you point your camera at a plane to see what it is. I would definitely recommend this also if you're interested about knowing your upcoming flight's ETA, and if there will be a delay. 10/10, would recommend.

Phenomenal app. Great for plane spotters and people picking people up from the airport alike! The one feature that I think would be cool would be a proximity alert notification for flights to let you know when a certain flight is within so many miles of your current position, it alerts you. (I definitely just want this so I know when certain planes are about to fly over my office so I can go and gawk!)

Free version best I have used. When somebody we know is flying. I track their flight from takeoff, in-flight and landing, including actual departure, arrival and in-flight times. No wondering pickup time it's there on Flightradar24. This app is so good I purchased the Gold version and that adds a whole new list of features that I dig and use often. We have family and friends using free, others 1 of the pay versions. Worth checking it out. I think your going to like.
